column1: Focus: full body, power, core, conditioning

column6: Total body stability/mobility. BJJ specific. Learn the pattern with an 8 kg dumbbell before the 20 kg bell.
Conventional pull replacing the hex bar. Bar against the shins; brace and push the floor away.
column7: Neutral spine — never round or lean back at lockout. Stay at RPE 7-8; never grind or test maxes. On a symptomatic week swap for the Sliding Bench leg press.

column6: Press up and forward along the bar arc. Shoulder-friendly and hits all 3 delt heads with real load.
Rear foot on the bench or step. Lighter weight continuous tension.
One-sided heavy carry replacing the farmers walk. Anti-lateral-flexion core work plus grip. Walk laps and switch hands each length.
Thoracic expansion. Serratus/Lat work. Hold the bell by the horns over the chest.
Anti-extension: press the low back to the floor; opposite arm/leg.
Anti-rotation: brace and press straight out; resist the pull.
Metabolic finisher: max-effort full-body reps; step back instead of jumping if form breaks.
